_README: Interpretive text for the study-1 public leaderboard page. THESE ARE CLAIMS ABOUT RESULTS — they are only true of the study-1 dev runs (config=frozen, dataset=study-1/datasets/dev). study-1 dropped difficulty tiers, has a near-empty fabrication split (4 unanswerable questions / 3 facts — too small for a rate), and reports fresh accuracy at the FACT level (variants aggregated per fact_id). export_site.py cross-checks the gate_failures roster against models-gatefail.txt. If a re-run changes the picture, rewrite this file.

headline_findings.label: The top five tie
headline_findings.detail: The top five are one statistical cluster — every 90% CI overlaps the leader's. At 80 facts this ranks tiers, not fine positions.

headline_findings.label: 2B keeps pace with 4B
headline_findings.detail: Qwen3.5-2B (1.3 GB) sits in the top cluster with its own 4B sibling — half the size, indistinguishable here.

headline_findings.label: A 1-bit 27B holds up
headline_findings.detail: Bonsai-27B at 1-bit (3.8 GB) lands in the top cluster — surprising for that bit-width.

headline_findings.label: Never got the tools
headline_findings.detail: Five of nine packages never received the search tools — their GGUF chat template dropped the schema, so they scored zero searches and are off the board.

config_notes.provider: Brave. The frozen config's search backend.
config_notes.tool_desc: Enriched tool descriptions: tell the model what the tool is for and to write short keyword queries.
config_notes.system_prompt: Shipped prompt with the current date injected ('Today's date is X').
config_notes.max_turns: A five-turn agentic budget; the passers average well under two turns per question.
config_notes.enable_thinking: Off. Reasoning mode is disabled so every model is compared on the same footing.

metric_definitions.label: Fresh ✓
metric_definitions.description: Primary score. Share of FACTS answered correctly on the fresh (retrieval-required) split. Each fact has several phrasing variants; a fact's score is the mean correctness across its variants, and the reported rate is the mean over the 80 fresh facts. The 90% CI is a Wilson interval over 80 facts. These questions are about events after the models' training cutoff, so they cannot be answered from memory.

metric_definitions.label: Stable ✓
metric_definitions.description: Share of timeless general-knowledge facts answered correctly (same fact-level aggregation). Checks that adding web search does not damage what a model already knew.

metric_definitions.label: Searched
metric_definitions.description: Fraction of fresh questions on which the model actually issued at least one search. The dominant failure mode for small models is not searching at all.

metric_definitions.label: Size
metric_definitions.description: On-disk size of the exact GGUF weights run (decimal GB). A single proxy for the model's memory and compute footprint — params × quantisation in one number — and the x-axis of the accuracy-vs-size Pareto view. Not a claim about phone storage.

gate_failures.title: Not ranked — never received the search tools
gate_failures.mechanism: These packages' GGUF chat template doesn't render the tool block, so the search schema was never passed to the model (schema_not_rendered on all 399 fresh questions, zero searches). A packaging failure, not a capability verdict — they were never offered the tools, so they are listed, not ranked.

limitations: Dev split only: 80 fresh facts (399 variants). Neighbouring models are within noise; separating the top cluster needs ~400 facts (growable).
No fabrication rate — the unanswerable split is only 4 questions / 3 facts.
Scope is crisp-fact retrieval — not long-form synthesis or multi-hop depth.
Sealed holdout (58 facts) unused: config was frozen before any dev run, so dev is itself the out-of-sample test.
Graded by an LLM judge (deepseek-v4-flash) against golds verified by a disjoint frontier panel (gpt-5.6-luna + glm-5.2) — machine-verified, not human-graded.
Workstation run of the phone GGUF files — battery, heat and latency unmeasured.
bonsai-27b-q1 is 1-bit; others Q4_K_M except Qwen3-1.7B (Q6_K).
